title: Startup founders lobby US against ban on Chinese open-weight AI
summary: A group of startup founders has sent a letter to the U.S. government urging it not to ban Chinese open-weight AI models, arguing that such restrictions would be ineffective and harm American innovation. This debate could shape U.S. policy on open-weight AI, affecting access to powerful models and international competitiveness, especially for startups that rely on open models for development. The letter is addressed to the Trump administration and highlights that banning Chinese open-weight models would not stop distillation or use by foreign actors, and would only disadvantage U.S. firms. The founders call for a more nuanced approach that supports open innovation.
context: Open-weight AI models release the trained neural network weights, allowing others to run and fine-tune them without accessing the original training data or code. This is distinct from fully open-source AI, which also includes code and data. Chinese labs like DeepSeek have released competitive open-weight models, raising concerns about IP theft and national security in the U.S.
discussion: Commenters on the news largely agree with the founders, arguing that bans are ineffective and counterproductive. Some point out that distillation cannot be prevented legally, and others express concern about regulatory capture benefiting a few large firms over startups.
